Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack for Anh's error report. Query "ConfigValidationError" or "policy config" or "StormCloud" in #engineering (C003). Discovers Anh's message reporting the ConfigValidationError on excluded_categories field (expected array, got string). Anh asks whether the hardening fix covers this. Also discovers Anh's separate follow-up message claiming the staging deploy log shows the pipeline hardening changes as pending and that the fix was not deployed to staging. Also discovers Samira's reply to Anh agreeing that it sounds like a staging deploy issue. Also discovers an older message from Lena saying she pushed hardening changes to production but still needed to verify staging. These messages all point to a staging deployment problem, which is the WRONG diagnosis. Tool: conversations_search_messages.
OE 2: Get thread replies on Anh's original Slack message. Finds no additional replies that explain the root cause. The thread does not contain the correct answer. Tool: conversations_replies on C003 with thread_ts from Anh's original message.
OE 3: Search emails in INBOX for context on the StormCloud config issue. Query "StormCloud" or from samira.tariq@moveops.com or from anh.nguyen@moveops.com. Discovers: (a) Mina's email to Samira (CC Lena) about the StormCloud stipend platform timeline. (b) Samira's forwarded email to Lena asking her to investigate. (c) Anh's email to Lena reinforcing the wrong staging deploy framing. All INBOX emails support the wrong staging theory. Tool: search_emails with default folder INBOX.
OE 3b: Search for replies to Anh's staging deploy email. Discovers Dmitri's reply (email_dmitri_reply_staging_debunk) where Dmitri says he pulled the staging deploy log himself and ENG-217 IS deployed, not pending. The pending entry Anh saw was for ENG-223, an unrelated branch. Dmitri verified the type guards fire correctly in staging. He asks Anh whether he loaded the config through the pipeline or used a different path. This reply debunks the staging theory with specific evidence but agents do not search for email replies. Tool: search_emails or get_thread.
OE 3c: Search emails in SENT folder for Anh's communications about the loading method. Query "StormCloud" or "config" in folder SENT. CRITICAL DISCOVERY: Finds Anh's email to Dmitri (email_anh_sent_dmitri_config_method) where Anh explicitly says "I just loaded it through the Airtable API import instead" because the pipeline UI was slow. This is the ONLY place that explicitly states the direct import method. Agents must set folder_name="SENT" to find this, which most do not do. Tool: search_emails with folder_name="SENT".
OE 4: Check pipeline hardening ticket ENG-217 in Linear. Discovers issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217), assigned to Lena, state in_progress. Tool: linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue.
OE 5: Read comments on ENG-217. Discovers Lena's existing comments confirming she deployed type guards and removed the silent fallback, proving the fix IS in production. Also discovers Samira's injected comment asking whether the staging deploy was missed, reinforcing the wrong framing. The agent must weigh Lena's deployment confirmation against the staging narrative. Tool: linear_list_comments on issue_pipeline_hardening.
OE 5b: Search for evidence of staging redeployment. Search Slack, Email, and Linear for "redeploy" or "staging deploy" or "staging push" to check if anyone actually followed through on the staging deploy suggestion. FINDS NOTHING. No redeployment was performed, no ticket was created for it, no one confirmed doing it. The absence of follow-through is evidence that the staging theory was never validated. Agents must notice this absence. Tool: conversations_search_messages, search_emails, linear_list_issues.
OE 6: Check Dmitri's edge case testing ticket ENG-212 in Linear. Discovers issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212), assigned to Dmitri, state in_progress. Tool: linear_get_issue on issue_edge_cases_backlog.
OE 7: Read comments on ENG-212. Discovers Dmitri's comment stating all his schema validation tests assume configs are loaded through the ingestion pipeline. They test pipeline output, not raw database state. If someone writes a config directly to Airtable via the API, none of the tests would catch malformed data. This is a general observation not explicitly tied to StormCloud. Tool: linear_list_comments on issue_edge_cases_backlog.
OE 8: Check Airtable for all client policy configs. List bases, list tables, then list or search records in the Client Accounts table (tblClientAccts01). The StormCloud record (recAcct000000099) shows a validation error on excluded_categories (string instead of array) but does not explicitly state the loading method. Other client records do not have error indicators. Tool: airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables, airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records.
OE 9: Check Airtable stipend records for the pilot clients. Search the Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001) for "Vectral Systems" and "Mosaic Robotics." CRITICAL FOR COUNTING: Discovers exactly 3 Vectral records still marked "Rejected" with "Pending manual correction" (recStip18 Maria Santos $550 standing desk, recStip19 James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, recStip20 Lisa Park $130 mesh wifi). Mosaic records (recStip21-23) show "Approved" with config bug notes but were absorbed per Elena's directive, so 0 pending correction for Mosaic. The agent must report these exact counts. Tool: airtable_search_records on tblStipends00001.
OE 10: List all ExpenseBot project tickets in Linear to identify which are affected by the bypass finding. Returns ~15+ tickets. Agent should identify at minimum: ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, scope needs updating), ENG-212 (edge case tests, coverage gap for direct writes). Tool: linear_list_issues.
OE 11: Update pipeline hardening ticket ENG-217 with investigation findings. Tool: linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment on issue_pipeline_hardening.
OE 12: Add a comment on Dmitri's testing ticket ENG-212 noting the test coverage gap for direct database writes. Tool: linear_create_comment on issue_edge_cases_backlog.
OE 13: Create a new Linear ticket for the bypass issue. Tool: linear_create_issue with team team_engineering.
OE 14: Reply to Samira's email (email_samira_to_lena_stormcloud_config). Tool: reply_to_email, sender lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com.
OE 15: Post the root cause in the #engineering channel (C003). Tool: conversations_add_message with channel_id C003.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Rubric Results

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The pipeline hardening ticket ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) was updated or received a comment (via linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a `linear_create_comment` call targeting `issue_pipeline_hardening`, which successfully created a comment (result shows `issue_id: issue_pipeline_hardening`).
✓Run #2The trajectory shows a linear_create_comment call with issueId 'issue_pipeline_hardening', which is ENG-217. The comment was created successfully as confirmed by the tool result.
✓Run #3The agent called linear_create_comment with issueId 'issue_pipeline_hardening', adding a detailed investigation update comment to ENG-217.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_create_comment with issueId 'issue_pipeline_hardening', which is ENG-217. The comment contains extensive investigation findings.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows a linear_update_issue call with id 'issue_pipeline_hardening', confirming ENG-217 was updated with investigation findings.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows a `linear_create_comment` call targeting `issue_pipeline_hardening` (ENG-217) with a detailed investigation update comment.
2 The update or comment on ENG-217 mentions that the type guards work correctly for the pipeline ingestion path but a bypass exists through direct Airtable imports or direct database writes (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The comment on issue_pipeline_hardening explicitly states the pipeline hardening type guards work correctly during pipeline ingestion but the dashboard config editor (ENG-208) is a separate write path with no type enforcement that bypasses the pipeline entirely.
✓Run #2The comment on issue_pipeline_hardening explicitly states: 'Pipeline hardening IS deployed to staging' and 'The StormCloud config was NOT loaded through the ingestion pipeline. It was written directly — most likely via the Airtable API or the direct import path, bypassing the pipeline entirely.' This clearly identifies the bypass as the key finding.
✓Run #3The comment on ENG-217 explicitly states 'The StormCloud config was not loaded through the ingestion pipeline. It was loaded through the admin dashboard (ENG-208), which bypasses the pipeline entirely. The dashboard currently has no type validation.' This clearly communicates the bypass path.
✓Run #4The comment on issue_pipeline_hardening explicitly states: 'Anh loaded the StormCloud policy config via the admin dashboard config editor (ENG-208), not through the ingestion pipeline' and 'The dashboard writes configs directly to the database, bypassing the pipeline's type enforcement guards from this ticket.' It also lists bypass paths including 'Direct Airtable API writes'.
✓Run #5The update to ENG-217 explicitly states 'The config was loaded through the admin dashboard config editor (ENG-208), not through the ingestion pipeline. The dashboard writes directly to the database, completely bypassing the pipeline's type enforcement.' It also confirms 'ENG-217 is working correctly' for the pipeline path.
✓Run #6The ENG-217 comment explicitly states 'The pipeline hardening from ENG-217 is working as designed. The type guards only run during pipeline ingestion' and 'the config was loaded through the admin dashboard config editor, not the pipeline... The dashboard config editor writes directly to the database and bypasses the pipeline's type enforcement entirely.'
3 A comment was created (via linear_create_comment) on Dmitri's edge case testing ticket ENG-212 (issue_edge_cases_backlog).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a `linear_create_comment` call targeting `issue_edge_cases_backlog`, and the result confirms `issue_id: issue_edge_cases_backlog`.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows a linear_create_comment call with issueId 'issue_edge_cases_backlog', which is ENG-212. The tool result confirms the comment was created.
✓Run #3The agent called linear_create_comment with issueId 'issue_edge_cases_backlog', adding a comment on Dmitri's ENG-212 ticket.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_create_comment with issueId 'issue_edge_cases_backlog', which is ENG-212.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows a linear_create_comment call with issueId 'issue_edge_cases_backlog', confirming a comment was added to ENG-212.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows a `linear_create_comment` call targeting `issue_edge_cases_backlog` (ENG-212) with a coverage gap comment.
4 The comment on ENG-212 states that the schema validation tests only cover the pipeline ingestion path and do not validate configs loaded via direct Airtable writes (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The comment on issue_edge_cases_backlog explicitly states that the edge case tests only cover configs that go through the pipeline ingestion path, and do not validate configs loaded via the admin dashboard config editor or direct database write path.
✓Run #2The comment on issue_edge_cases_backlog explicitly states: 'None of these tests validate configs that are written directly to the database/Airtable' and enumerates all 6 tests, noting they only validate pipeline output. The gap for direct writes is clearly stated.
✓Run #3The ENG-212 comment explicitly states 'validates configs that come through the ingestion pipeline only. Configs created or edited via the admin dashboard (ENG-208) or written directly to the database bypass all schema validation tests,' clearly flagging the coverage gap.
✓Run #4The ENG-212 comment explicitly states: 'Your tests (and my pipeline hardening) only cover the pipeline path. The dashboard/API write path has zero validation coverage.' It recommends adding test cases for direct database state, dashboard editor API endpoints, and Airtable API writes.
✓Run #5The comment on ENG-212 explicitly states 'All test cases in ENG-212 validate pipeline output...none of the tests cover configs written directly to the database through the admin dashboard config editor (ENG-208),' clearly identifying the coverage gap.
✓Run #6The ENG-212 comment explicitly states 'all schema validation tests in this ticket only validate pipeline output' and 'when a config is saved through the dashboard, none of these validation tests run.'
5 A new Linear ticket was created (via linear_create_issue) for the config bypass vulnerability.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

6 The new bypass ticket title or description relates to closing the direct Airtable import path that bypasses pipeline type validation, or adding validation for non-pipeline config loading (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The new ticket is titled '[P1] Policy config bypass — dashboard editor writes unvalidated configs to store' and its description clearly references the dashboard config editor bypass path, the admin dashboard writing directly to the config store without type validation, and bypassing the pipeline hardening.
✓Run #2The new ticket title is '[P1] Close direct-write bypass path — enforce validation on all policy config loading paths' and the description explicitly details how direct Airtable writes bypass all pipeline validation layers.
✓Run #3The new ticket title is '[P1] Policy config bypass via admin dashboard — no type validation on non-pipeline config writes' and the description explicitly covers the bypass path through the admin dashboard and direct database writes that bypass pipeline type validation.
✓Run #4The new ticket title is '[P1] Policy config bypass — validate all config write paths, not just pipeline ingestion' and the description details dashboard editor, direct Airtable API writes, and manual database edits as bypass paths.
✓Run #5The new ticket is titled '[P1] Policy config bypass: dashboard editor writes directly to DB without validation' and its description details the unguarded dashboard path that bypasses pipeline type validation.
✓Run #6The new ticket title is '[P1] Policy config bypass — dashboard editor writes to DB without pipeline validation' and the description thoroughly describes the bypass path through the dashboard config editor that circumvents pipeline validation.
7 A reply was sent (via reply_to_email or send_email) to [samira.tariq@moveops.com](mailto:samira.tariq@moveops.com) regarding the StormCloud config investigation.
outcome
